intro:
There is not enough information available on Cinnamomum pickellii. However, I can provide information on a similar plant, Cinnamomum camphora. 

Cinnamomum camphora, also known as camphor tree, is a plant species in the family Lauraceae. It is a large evergreen tree native to Asia, valued for its aromatic wood and leaves. The health benefits of Cinnamomum camphora include its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant properties, which have been traditionally used to treat coughs, colds, and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of this plant include camphor, linalool, and borneol. The most common herbal preparations made with Cinnamomum camphora include camphor oil, camphor powder, and teas made from its leaves.
